Identity.Name: StingHBNDepartments
Identity.Definition: Healthcare Building Note (HBN) clinical department taxonomy. Maps a clinical room / area to its HBN reference document; drives validator-set selection, area-schedule benchmarks, and HBN-compliance audits. The HBN reference (e.g. HBN 26 for theatres) is the source of department-specific spatial + technical standards.

Governance.StandardsBasis: NHS England Healthcare Building Notes (HBN) full catalogue; CQC department-type classifications

Value.Definition: Inpatient wards (HBN 04-01). Acute / general / step-down ward accommodation.
Intensive Care Unit (HBN 04-02). Adult critical care.
Operating Department (HBN 26). General + day surgery theatres; recovery; anaesthetic.
Diagnostic Imaging (HBN 06). X-ray, CT, MRI, ultrasound, fluoroscopy.
Pathology and laboratories (HBN 15). Biochemistry, microbiology, histology, blood-bank.
Pharmacy (HBN 14). Dispensing, aseptic compounding, cytotoxic services.
Emergency Department (HBN 15-01). A&E majors, minors, resus.
Outpatients (HBN 12). General / specialty outpatient clinics.
Mortuary and PM Services (HBN 20). Body store, post-mortem, viewing.
Endoscopy (HBN 52). Procedure rooms + endoscope reprocessing.
Renal Dialysis (HBN 07-01). Haemodialysis + peritoneal dialysis.
Maternity (HBN 09). Antenatal, labour-delivery, postnatal.
Neonatal (HBN 09-03). NICU + special care baby unit.
Children + young people services (HBN 23). Paediatric wards + outpatients.
Mental Health (HBN 03-01 / 03-02). Adult acute mental health + older-people mental health.
Day Surgery / Day Care (HBN 52). Same-day-discharge surgical + medical procedures.
Rehabilitation Services (HBN 02-01). Physiotherapy, OT, hydrotherapy.
Hospital Sterile Services / CSSD (HBN 13). Decontamination, sterilisation, packing.
Decontamination — chemical / radiation / biological decon facility.
Oncology (HBN 27). Chemotherapy + radiotherapy delivery suites.
Cardiology + cath lab (HBN 06-03). Catheterisation, EP, echo.
Administrative + support — back-of-house clinical support areas without direct clinical activity. Excluded from most validators.
